expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid banding scenarios distinguish styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the benchmark focus and enumerates the three required distinctions: “Modern grid banding scenarios that distinguish: - Styling variants … - Interactions … - Backward-compatible rendering outcomes …”. It also reiterates it cannot verify the plan due to missing Phase 4a artifacts, but the case focus itself is explicitly covered in the output text.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: Both execution_notes.md and result.md explicitly target Phase 4a and check Phase 4a contract artifacts: execution_notes.md: “Primary phase under test: Phase 4a (subcategory-only draft)” and “Checks performed vs phase4a contract…”. result.md: “output must be aligned to Phase 4a: a subcategory-only QA draft…” and it evaluates compliance against required Phase 4a artifacts (draft + spawn manifest).
